Our mission

Research shouldn’t be gatekept.

We believe any curious student can do real research, not just the ones with the right connections. Sign up to get started, or get involved as a mentor, contributor, or volunteer.

Start Here Get Involved
The myth

Research doesn’t only happen in a lab.

When people picture research, they picture a lab bench and a white coat. That picture is real, but it’s also the version that depends on who you know.

The picture in your head

Behind a door someone has to open

Lab placements usually run on access: a parent in the field, a family friend at a university, a school with the right connections. If you don’t have those, the door mostly stays shut, and it can feel like research isn’t for you.

Where you can start today

A desk, a laptop, a question

Real research also starts at home. Published papers, open datasets, and free tools are sitting online right now. Reading, questioning, and analysing them is genuine research, not practice for it.

You don’t need a lab coat to begin. You need a question, and somewhere honest to look for the answer.

Published research

Millions of papers are free to read on open-access journals and preprint servers.

The data behind it

Those papers publish their datasets, and public archives hold thousands more.

Your own analysis

Free tools let you question that data, test an idea, and find something new in it.

A finding worth sharing

Write it up and submit it. Student journals and competitions are open to you.

Questions

Before you start

Do I need top grades or a special school?

No. Research rewards curiosity and persistence more than grades. Our guides assume you're starting from zero and walk you through every step in order.

Does any of this cost money?

No. Everything here is free to read, and the methods we recommend rely on free tools and publicly available resources wherever possible.

I don't have a lab or fancy equipment. Can I still do research?

Yes. Many strong high-school projects use public datasets, surveys, code, or analysis of existing work, none of which require a lab. We'll help you pick a method that fits what you have.

How do I actually begin?

Head to the Start Here page. It gives you the big picture and a concrete first task you can finish today.
